nodes.label: traveller | be | journey
nodes.sentence:
Gulistan chapter II - story 5

Several travellers were on a journey together and equally sharing each other's troubles and comforts.

nodes.label: i | desire | ∅
nodes.sentence: I desired to accompany them but they would not agree.

nodes.label: i | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then I said: 'It is foreign to the manners of great men to turn away the face from the company of the poor and so deprive themselves of the advantage they might derive therefrom because I for one consider myself sufficiently strong and energetic to be of service to men and not an encumbrance.

nodes.label: i | aid | you
nodes.sentence: Although I am not riding on a beast, I shall aid you in carrying blankets.'

nodes.label: one | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
One of them said: 'Do not be grieved at the words thou hast heard because some days ago a thief in the guise of a dervish arrived and joined our company.'

How can people know who is in the dress?

nodes.label: writer | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The writer is aware what the book contains.

nodes.label: they | entertain | suspicion
nodes.sentence: As the state of dervishes is safe, they entertained no suspicion about him and received him as a friend.

nodes.label: state | be | dress
nodes.sentence: The outward state of Arifs is the patched dress.

nodes.label: it | suffice | display
nodes.sentence: It suffices as a display to the face of the people.

nodes.label: ∅ | strive | act
nodes.sentence: Strive by thy acts to be good and wear anything thou listest.

nodes.label: abandoning | be | sanctity
nodes.sentence:
The abandoning of the world, of lust, and of desire
Is sanctity, not the abandonment of the robe only.

nodes.label: it | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: It is necessary to show manhood in the fight.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | weapon
nodes.sentence: Of what profit are weapons of war to an hermaphrodite?

nodes.label: we | travel | ∅
nodes.sentence: We travelled one day till the night set in during which we slept near a fort and the graceless thief, taking up the water-pot of a companion, pretending to go for an ablution, departed for plunder.

nodes.label: saint | make | covering
nodes.sentence:
A pretended saint who wears the dervish garb
Has made of the Ka'bah's robes the covering of an ass.

nodes.label: he | go | tower
nodes.sentence: After disappearing from the sight of the dervishes, he went to a tower from which he stole a casket and, when the day dawned, the dark-hearted wretch had already progressed a considerable distance.

nodes.label: guiltless | take | morning
nodes.sentence: In the morning the guiltless sleeping companions were all taken to the fort and thrown into prison.

nodes.label: we | renounce | companionship
nodes.sentence: From that date we renounced companionship and took the road of solitude, according to the maxim: Safety is in solitude.

nodes.label: honour | leave | low
nodes.sentence:
When one of a tribe has done a foolish thing
No honour is left either to the low or the high.

nodes.label: i | exclude | advantage
nodes.sentence: I replied: 'Thanks be to the God of majesty and glory, I have not been excluded from the advantages enjoyed by dervishes, although I have separated myself from their society.

nodes.label: i | profit | ∅
nodes.sentence: I have profited by what thou hast narrated to me and this admonition will be of use through life to persons like me.'

nodes.label: ∅ | grieve | fellow
nodes.sentence:
For one rude fellow in the assembly
The heart of intelligent men is much grieved.

nodes.label: ∅ | pollute | whole
nodes.sentence:
If a tank be filled with rose-water
A dog falling into it pollutes the whole.
